lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: Hello. My name is Dimintheis, of the noble family Fitzharmon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why would a nobleman live in a dump like this?

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: The King has taken my estate from me until such time as I can show my family crest to him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why would he do that?

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: Well, there is a long standing rule of chivalry amongst the Varrockian aristocracy, where each noble family is in possession of a unique crest, which signifies the honour and lineage of the family. More than this

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: however, it also represents the lawful rights of each family to prove their ownership of their wealth and lands. If the family crest is lost, then the family's estate is handed over to the current monarch until the crest is

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: restored.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: This dates back to the times when there was much in- fighting amongst the noble families and their clans, and was introduced as a way of reducing the bloodshed that was devestating the ranks of the ruling classes at that

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: time. When you captured a rival family's clan, you also captured their lands and wealth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So where is this crest?

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: Well, my three sons took it with them many years ago when they rode out to fight in the war against the undead necromancer and his army in the battle to save Varrock.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: For many years I had assumed them all dead, as I had heard no word from them, but recently I heard word that my son Caleb is alive and well, and trying to earn his fortune as a great chef in the lands beyond White

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: Wolf Mountain, although I know not where.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I will help you.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: I thank you greatly adventurer! Also... if you find Caleb... please... let him know his father still loves him...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not interested in that adventure right now.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: I realise it was a lot to ask of a stranger.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're rich then? Can I have some money?

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: Gah! Lousy beggar! Your sort is what's ruining this great land! Why don't you just go and get a job if you need money so badly?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, I am a bold adventurer.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: An adventurer hmmm? How lucky. I may have an adventure for you. I desperately need my family crest returning to me. It is of utmost importance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are you so desperate for it?

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: Well, there is a long standing rule of chivalry amongst the Varrockian aristocracy, where each noble family is in possession of a unique crest, which signifies the honour and lineage of the family. More than this

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: however, it also represents the lawful rights of each family to prove their ownership of their wealth and lands. If the family crest is lost, then the family's estate is handed over to the current monarch until the crest is

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: restored.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: This dates back to the times when there was much in- fighting amongst the noble families and their clans, and was introduced as a way of reducing the bloodshed that was devestating the ranks of the ruling classes at that

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: time. When you captured a rival family's clan, you also captured their lands and wealth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So where is this crest?

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: Well, my three sons took it with them many years ago when they rode out to fight in the war against the undead necromancer and his army in the battle to save Varrock.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: For many years I had assumed them all dead, as I had heard no word from them, but recently I heard word that my son Caleb is alive and well, and trying to earn his fortune as a great chef in the lands beyond White

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: Wolf Mountain, although I know not where.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not interested in that adventure right now.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: I realise it was a lot to ask of a stranger.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any quests for me? I really want one. I really want to help out!

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: My family consider what you did for us a great service. We couldn't possibly trouble you further.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There must be SOMETHING you need help with. I am a great adventurer after all.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: There really isn't anything, I am very content with life right now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess money really does buy happiness.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: What was that? I didn't quite catch that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh come on, surely there's something I can help out with! I want to help, however menial it is.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: Okay okay. Fine then. Whilst I don't have anything for you, how about you try my sons, they are always wanting something done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fantastic! I'll get straight to it!

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: Hello again, adventurer. I hope that Shield of Arrav business marks the start of something big for my family. Maybe it will help me finally get out of this hole!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any gauntlets for me?

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: Yes, your [steel/goldsmithing/cooking/chaos] gauntlets returned to me through their magic power recently. Would you like them back?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: Here you go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not right now.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: Well, come back if you change your mind any time. I owe you for your trouble.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: No, I don't think so. You must have them yourself somewhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Dimintheis.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: I will not forget what you did for my family, [player name]. Those 500,000 coins have allowed us to put food on the table.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's no problem.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: Combine that with the whole Shield of Arrav business and things are really starting to look up for us!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I get some more gauntlets, please?

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: You may, but you'll have to ask my sons for them, unless you just want the plain steel gauntlets.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I do want some steel gauntlets, please.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: Here you go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks, I'll ask your sons instead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I thought these gauntlets were a family heirloom. How do you have several of them?

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: I'm sorry for lying to you, [player name], it was just all part of the ploy to make our family seem wealthy.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: They're not heirlooms at all, we have crates full of the things hidden away in the basement.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you still think that these are an acceptable reward for what I have done for your family?

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: It's all I have to give...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very well, I do admit, they are useful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: See you another time, Dimintheis.
